397	          # FEATURES.md before it may commit/build/publish. Fail CLOSED: no
398	          # parsable verdict (rate limit, malformed output) aborts rather than
399	          # ships an unreviewed merge; a BREAKAGE verdict hard-aborts with the
400	          # findings in the log (baseline untouched -> recovery loop).
401	          echo "::group::semantic feature review (Opus)"
402	          npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
403	          REVIEW_FILE="$RUNNER_TEMP/merge-review.txt"
404	          rm -f "$REVIEW_FILE"
405	          # The reviewer must not modify the tree; snapshot the work-tree state
406	          # and refuse to continue if the review run changed ANYTHING.
407	          STATUS_BEFORE=$(git status --porcelain=v1 | sha256sum)
408	          REVIEW_PROMPT="IMPORTANT: Do NOT enter plan mode. You are reviewing an ALREADY-RESOLVED, staged, uncommitted merge of upstream superset tag ${NEWTAG} into our Windows-ARM64 fork. Your ONLY job is to decide whether the merged result SEMANTICALLY breaks any fork feature. Steps: (1) Read FEATURES.md — the fork feature manifest (feature descriptions + marker tokens). (2) Run 'git --no-pager diff --cached --name-only' to list every file the merge changed. (3) For each changed file that participates in a fork feature — it contains a FEATURES.md marker token, or it is in the blast radius of one (e.g. a projection/select/mapping that a feature's fields flow through, a component a feature's props pass through, a hook a feature's guard lives in) — read the staged diff ('git --no-pager diff --cached -- <file>') AND the surrounding file content, and verify the fork behaviour still works end-to-end: fork-added fields still projected/carried, fork branches and guards still reachable, fork props still passed, fork call sites still invoked. An upstream refactor that preserves behaviour is fine; flag only real breakage. (4) Write your verdict to the file at ${REVIEW_FILE} — the FIRST line must be exactly 'VERDICT: OK' or 'VERDICT: BREAKAGE'; for BREAKAGE add one line per finding: '<file> — <feature/marker> — <what broke>'. (5) If the file at ${WFDELTA_FILE} is non-empty, read it — it is upstream's CI/workflow delta we deliberately did NOT merge (.github/workflows is fork-owned). If it moves BUILD-RELEVANT logic into upstream CI (a codegen/generate step, a newly required env var, a packaging/signing step) that our fork's own build workflow would now silently miss, append one line per item after the verdict: 'CI-NOTE: <what upstream CI now does that the fork build may need>'. CI-NOTEs alone never change the verdict. Rules: do NOT edit ANY repository file; do NOT run git add / commit / checkout / merge / restore; only read files, run read-only git diff/log/show commands, and write the verdict file. Output nothing else."
409	          attempt=1
410	          while [ $attempt -le 2 ]; do
411	            echo "::group::claude review attempt $attempt"
412	            claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model opus --effort high -p "$REVIEW_PROMPT" || true
413	            echo "::endgroup::"
414	            if [ -s "$REVIEW_FILE" ] && head -1 "$REVIEW_FILE" | grep -qE '^VERDICT: (OK|BREAKAGE)$'; then break; fi
415	            attempt=$((attempt+1))
416	          done
417	          STATUS_AFTER=$(git status --porcelain=v1 | sha256sum)
418	          if [ "$STATUS_BEFORE" != "$STATUS_AFTER" ]; then
419	            echo "::error::(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) the review run modified the work tree — refusing to trust or commit it. Baseline untouched."
420	            exit 1
421	          fi
422	          if ! [ -s "$REVIEW_FILE" ] || ! head -1 "$REVIEW_FILE" | grep -qE '^VERDICT: (OK|BREAKAGE)$'; then
423	            echo "::error::(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) no parsable review verdict for $NEWTAG — refusing to ship an UNREVIEWED merge (fail closed). Baseline untouched."
424	            exit 1
425	          fi
426	          echo "--- review verdict ---"
427	          cat "$REVIEW_FILE"
428	          if head -1 "$REVIEW_FILE" | grep -q 'BREAKAGE'; then
429	            echo "::error::(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) Opus found fork-feature breakage in the $NEWTAG merge — rejecting. Findings above. Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline."
430	            exit 1
431	          fi
432	          echo "::endgroup::"
433	
434	          # --- record new baseline + commit the merge -------------------------
435	          # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) The release tag is derived SOLELY from
436	          # apps/desktop/package.json by the build — if the merged version
437	          # doesn't match the tag being merged, the build would delete and
438	          # rebuild the WRONG release (e.g. a bad package.json conflict
439	          # resolution keeping 1.14.3 while merging desktop-v1.15.0), then
440	          # advance the baseline past a version that no longer has a correct
441	          # release. Assert the invariant here instead.
442	          VERSION=$(node -p "require('./apps/desktop/package.json').version")
443	          if [ "desktop-v${VERSION}" != "$NEWTAG" ]; then
444	            echo "::error::merged apps/desktop/package.json version ($VERSION) does not match $NEWTAG — bad version resolution; refusing to commit. Baseline untouched."
445	            exit 1
446	          fi
447	          # (WORKFLOW-FORK-OWNED) postcondition: the tree about to be committed
448	          # must contain ZERO workflow-dir changes — not from the merge, not
449	          # from the AI resolver, not from the review run. The restore step
450	          # above is the mechanism; this is the proof (a workflow delta would be
451	          # rejected by the push anyway — fail here, loud, with the baseline
452	          # untouched).
453	          if [ -n "$(git status --porcelain=v1 --untracked-files=all -- .github/workflows)" ]; then
454	            echo "::error::(WORKFLOW-FORK-OWNED) .github/workflows differs from HEAD after resolve/review — refusing to commit:"
455	            git status --porcelain=v1 --untracked-files=all -- .github/workflows
456	            exit 1
457	          fi
458	          printf '%s\n' "$NEWTAG" > .fork/upstream-baseline.txt
459	          git add -A
460	          git commit -m "merge upstream ${NEWTAG}" -m "Auto-merged by nightly-merge; conflicts resolved by Opus where present; all FEATURES.md markers verified present; merged delta passed the (MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) Opus feature review."
461	          # (CANDIDATE-IDEMPOTENT-PUSH) The candidate is a disposable CI branch:
462	          # re-derived from main each run, consumed by `build`, deleted by
463	          # `advance` ON SUCCESS only. A FAILED build never reaches `advance`, so
464	          # it strands the candidate on the remote. The next night re-merges the
465	          # SAME upstream version into a NEW commit (AI conflict resolution is
466	          # non-deterministic AND main may have advanced), whose history diverges
467	          # from the stale branch — a plain push is then rejected non-fast-forward
468	          # and EVERY subsequent nightly deadlocks on that stale ref. Drop any
469	          # stale remote copy first so the push is always clean. This is pure CI
470	          # scratch (never a feature/history branch), so overwriting it is safe;
471	          # we delete-then-push rather than --force a real branch.
472	          # (REHEARSAL) Everything above — merge, resolver, repair, every
473	          # gate, even the local merge commit — ran for real; stop before
474	          # anything leaves this runner. This is the on-demand emulation of
475	          # the overnight run: after ANY change to this workflow, dispatch
476	          # rehearse=true (+ force_tag / rehearse_base to replay a past
477	          # night) and get tonight's answer today, with zero side effects.
478	          if [ "$REHEARSE" = "true" ]; then
479	            echo "should_build=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
480	            {
481	              echo "## REHEARSAL PASSED — $NEWTAG"
482	              echo "Merge + conflict resolution + REFERR-REPAIR + marker gate + dependency gates + semantic review all green."
483	              echo "Merge commit (local only, discarded): $(git rev-parse HEAD)"
484	              echo "No candidate pushed, no build, no advance, baseline untouched."
485	            } | tee -a "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
486	            exit 0
487	          fi
488	          git push origin --delete "$CAND" 2>/dev/null || true
489	          git push origin "$CAND"
490	
491	          echo "should_build=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
492	          echo "newtag=$NEWTAG" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
493	          echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
494	          echo "candidate=$CAND" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
495	          # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) build + advance consume this immutable SHA, not
496	          # the mutable branch name — a replaced candidate can't be built or
497	          # advanced unnoticed.
498	          echo "sha=$(git rev-parse HEAD)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
499	          echo "Merged $NEWTAG -> candidate $CAND @ $(git rev-parse HEAD) (version $VERSION). Building next."
500	
501	  build:
502	    needs: merge
503	    if: needs.merge.outputs.should_build == 'true'
504	    uses: ./.github/workflows/build-arm64.yml
505	    with:
506	      # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) build the exact merge commit, immune to candidate-
507	      # branch mutation between jobs.
508	      ref: ${{ needs.merge.outputs.sha }}
509	      publish: true
510	
511	  advance:
512	    needs: [merge, build]
513	    if: needs.merge.outputs.should_build == 'true'
514	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
515	    steps:
516	      - name: Checkout
517	        uses: actions/checkout@v5
518	        with:
519	          fetch-depth: 0
520	      - name: Fast-forward main to the verified candidate + clean up
521	        shell: bash
522	        run: |
523	          set -euo pipefail
524	          CAND='${{ needs.merge.outputs.candidate }}'
525	          SHA='${{ needs.merge.outputs.sha }}'
526	          git fetch origin "$CAND"
527	          # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) advance ONLY the commit that was gated + built.
528	          if [ "$(git rev-parse FETCH_HEAD)" != "$SHA" ]; then
529	            echo "::error::candidate $CAND moved (expected $SHA, found $(git rev-parse FETCH_HEAD)) — another run interfered; refusing to advance main."
530	            exit 1
531	          fi
532	          # The candidate is main + the merge commit, so this is a fast-forward.
533	          git push origin "$SHA:refs/heads/main"
534	          git push origin --delete "$CAND"
535	          echo "Baseline advanced to ${{ needs.merge.outputs.newtag }}; Release published by the build job."
536

file.content:
# carries ZERO upstream workflow files (upstream CI targets upstream's
          # infra/secrets and is meaningless here), and the Actions GITHUB_TOKEN
          # can NEVER push a .github/workflows/* change — GitHub rejects both
          # the candidate push and the main fast-forward with "refusing to allow
          # a GitHub App to create or update workflow … without `workflows`
          # permission" (desktop-v1.14.3 deploy-relay.yml incident: the merge,
          # gates and review all passed, then the push died). Restore our exact
          # workflow set BEFORE conflict detection: upstream-ADDED files are
          # dropped from index+worktree (--no-overlay), upstream edits to files
          # we long deleted resolve as ours, and the AI resolver never sees a
          # workflow path it could resurrect.
          #
          # UNMERGED paths must be cleared FIRST: `git checkout <tree> -- <dir>`
          # hard-errors ("path … is unmerged") on a modify/delete entry whose
          # file is absent in HEAD — upstream modifying a workflow we deleted
          # (desktop-v1.15.0 release-cli.yml incident: the nightly died right
          # here, before the resolver, two nights running). Resolve each
          # unmerged workflow path to OUR side: restore from HEAD when we carry
          # it, git rm when we deleted it.
          git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U -- .github/workflows | while IFS= read -r p; do
            if git cat-file -e "HEAD:$p" 2>/dev/null; then
              git checkout HEAD -- "$p"
            else
              git rm -q -f -- "$p"
            fi
          done
          git checkout --no-overlay HEAD -- .github/workflows

          CONFLICTS=$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U || true)
          if [ -n "$CONFLICTS" ]; then
            echo "::group::conflicts to resolve"; echo "$CONFLICTS"; echo "::endgroup::"
            npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
            PROMPT="IMPORTANT: Do NOT enter plan mode. You are inside a git repository mid-merge: upstream superset tag ${NEWTAG} is being merged into our Windows-ARM64 fork. ONLY the files listed by 'git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U' are conflicted. Resolve EVERY conflicted file: combine upstream's change with our fork's feature so BOTH intents survive and our feature keeps working; where upstream deliberately removed or refactored something we had only lightly touched, follow upstream. Do NOT edit any non-conflicted file. Do NOT weaken or remove any feature marker tracked in FEATURES.md. After resolving each file run 'git add <file>'. Do NOT commit. Do NOT run any other git command. When every conflict is resolved and staged, stop. Output nothing."
            attempt=1
            while [ $attempt -le 3 ]; do
              echo "::group::claude resolve attempt $attempt"
              claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model opus --effort high -p "$PROMPT" || true
              echo "::endgroup::"
              if [ -z "$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U || true)" ]; then break; fi
              attempt=$((attempt+1))
            done
            REMAIN=$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U || true)
            if [ -n "$REMAIN" ]; then
              echo "::error::Opus could not resolve all conflicts for $NEWTAG — recovery: fix locally and re-baseline. Remaining:"; echo "$REMAIN"
              exit 1
            fi
          else
            echo "Clean merge — no conflicts."
          fi

          # --- dependency-consistency gate + lock regen (LOCK-REGEN) ----------
          # The build installs --frozen-lockfile and electron-builder's
          # dependency traversal requires each workspace's DECLARED dep version
          # to be the one actually installed. A merge introduces two kinds of
          # drift that both kill the build ~25 min in (after a windows-arm64
          # runner spins up), not here:
          #   (1) a fork root `overrides` entry pins a package to a version
          #       DIFFERENT from the merged workspace pin (this is exactly how a
          #       stale {@mastra/core, mastracode} override broke every mastra
          #       bump: override forced 0.18.0, chat declared 0.18.1 ->
          #       electron-builder "production dependency not found 0.18.1"); and
          #   (2) the merge bumps a package.json dep but bun.lock still pins the
          #       old version (a stale frozen lock).
          # Catch (1) loud here (pure manifest analysis, platform-independent);
          # fix (2) by regenerating the lock with the build's exact bun version,
          # which ALSO fails loud if the merged manifests don't resolve or a pin
          # is age-blocked. Both run in this 2-min job, so the windows-arm64
          # build never spins up on a broken tree. We deliberately do NOT run a
          # full `--frozen-lockfile` install here: this is a linux runner, so a
          # platform-specific install/native-build quirk would be a false abort,
          # and after a successful --lockfile-only the lock is consistent with
          # the manifests by construction (same bun version the build uses).
          # Any failure below hard-aborts with the baseline untouched.
          echo "::group::dependency-consistency gate"
          node scripts/check-override-consistency.mjs
          echo "::endgroup::"

          echo "::group::regenerate bun.lock to match merged manifests"
          export SUPERSET_POSTINSTALL_RUNNING=1
          LOCKLOG="$RUNNER_TEMP/bun-lockfile.log"
          set +e
          bun install --lockfile-only > "$LOCKLOG" 2>&1
          rc=$?
          set -e
          cat "$LOCKLOG"
          if [ "$rc" -ne 0 ]; then
            if grep -q "blocked by minimum-release-age" "$LOCKLOG"; then
              echo "::error::Lock regen blocked by bunfig minimumReleaseAge — an upstream dep pin in $NEWTAG is <72h old. This is a timing race, not a breakage: the nightly will succeed once the version ages past the gate (re-run after it ages, or wait for tomorrow's schedule). Baseline left untouched."
            else
              echo "::error::bun install --lockfile-only failed (rc=$rc) after merging $NEWTAG — the merged manifests don't resolve. Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline."
            fi
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "::endgroup::"

          # --- (REFERR-REPAIR) run the build's ReferenceError gate HERE and let
          # Opus repair what it catches, so an import-dropping conflict
          # resolution self-heals tonight instead of failing the build and
          # costing a red night + manual recovery (desktop-v1.15.1
          # useChangesTab incident: the resolver took upstream's rewritten
          # import block, kept the fork's JSX, dropped Button/Tooltip/cn).
          # Repair is scoped HARD to cannot-find-name diagnostics; anything
          # still dirty after 2 attempts aborts exactly as before (the build
          # would have rejected it anyway — this just moves the gate earlier
          # and adds a bounded fix loop). --ignore-scripts keeps the linux
          # install pure-JS resolution (no native/postinstall platform quirks,
          # the reason this job deliberately avoids a full install elsewhere).
          echo "::group::(REFERR-REPAIR) ReferenceError gate + bounded AI repair"
          bun install --frozen-lockfile --ignore-scripts
          REFERRLOG="$RUNNER_TEMP/referr.log"
          run_referr_gate() {
            set +e
            node scripts/check-dangerous-diagnostics.mjs > "$REFERRLOG" 2>&1
            REFERR_RC=$?
            set -e
            cat "$REFERRLOG"
          }
          run_referr_gate
          # The repair agent must only EDIT the work tree — record the commit
          # state so an agent that disobeys "Do NOT commit" (advancing HEAD
          # and vacating the merge) is caught, not silently laundered into a
          # clean-looking tree.
          HEAD_BEFORE_REPAIR=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
          attempt=1
          while [ "$REFERR_RC" -ne 0 ] && [ $attempt -le 2 ]; do
            DIAGS=$(grep -F '(REFERR-GATE)' "$REFERRLOG" | head -40 || true)
            if [ -z "$DIAGS" ]; then
              # Gate failed WITHOUT naming diagnostics = the checker itself
              # broke (install/tsc infra). AI repair has nothing to act on.
              echo "::error::(REFERR-REPAIR) gate failed without diagnostics — checker infrastructure problem, not repairable. Log above. Baseline untouched."
              exit 1
            fi
            npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
            REPAIR_PROMPT="IMPORTANT: Do NOT enter plan mode. You are inside a git repository holding a staged, uncommitted merge of upstream superset tag ${NEWTAG}. The typecheck gate found cannot-find-name diagnostics — identifiers that would throw ReferenceError at runtime, almost always imports dropped during conflict resolution. Diagnostics: ${DIAGS} — Fix ONLY these diagnostics, and ONLY by ADDING code: add the missing import lines (match how the same symbols are imported elsewhere in this repo, e.g. @superset/ui/*) or restore the dropped declaration. NEVER delete, comment out, or rewrite existing code, JSX, or use-sites to silence a diagnostic — removing the code that uses the identifier is feature loss and is forbidden. Do NOT touch any file not named in the diagnostics. After editing each file run 'git add <file>'. Do NOT commit. Output nothing."
            echo "::group::claude referr-repair attempt $attempt"
            claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model opus --effort high -p "$REPAIR_PROMPT" || true
            echo "::endgroup::"
            run_referr_gate
            attempt=$((attempt+1))
          done
          if [ "$REFERR_RC" -ne 0 ]; then
            echo "::error::(REFERR-REPAIR) cannot-find-name diagnostics persist after repair attempts — aborting (the build would reject this tree). Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline. Baseline untouched."
            exit 1
          fi
          if [ "$(git rev-parse HEAD)" != "$HEAD_BEFORE_REPAIR" ] || ! git rev-parse -q --verify MERGE_HEAD >/dev/null; then
            echo "::error::(REFERR-REPAIR) the repair run committed or destroyed the merge state — refusing to trust it. Baseline untouched."
            exit 1
          fi
          # Stage any repair edits NOW: the semantic review below reads the
          # STAGED diff ('git diff --cached'); an unstaged repair would be
          # invisible to the review yet still swept into the final commit's
          # 'git add -A' — reviewed tree and shipped tree must be identical.
          git add -A
          echo "::endgroup::"

          # --- feature-marker gate (FEATURES.md) ------------------------------
          # Runs AFTER conflict resolution AND the REFERR repair loop, so it
          # judges the FINAL tree — a repair that "fixed" a diagnostic by
          # deleting fork code (forbidden, but prompts are not proofs) still
          # has to get past the marker grep here.
          echo "::group::feature-marker gate"
          # read the fenced ```markers block: lines are "<token><TAB><glob-root>"
          # Guard the guard: an empty/renamed block would pass VACUOUSLY (zero
          # markers checked == zero missing). Fail loud instead.
          MARKER_LINES=$(awk '/^```markers$/{f=1;next} /^```$/{f=0} f' FEATURES.md)
          if ! printf '%s\n' "$MARKER_LINES" | grep -q '[^[:space:]]'; then
            echo "::error::FEATURES.md markers block is missing or empty — the marker gate would pass vacuously. Refusing to trust this merge."
            exit 1
          fi
          printf '%s\n' "$MARKER_LINES" | while IFS=$'\t' read -r tok root; do
            [ -n "$tok" ] || continue
            if grep -rqsF -- "$tok" "$root" 2>/dev/null; then
              echo "  OK   $tok ($root)"
            else
              echo "::error::feature marker MISSING after merge: '$tok' under $root"
              echo missing >> "$RUNNER_TEMP/marker-missing"
            fi
          done
          echo "::endgroup::"
          if [ -f "$RUNNER_TEMP/marker-missing" ]; then
            echo "::error::A feature was dropped by the $NEWTAG merge — rejecting. Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline."
            exit 1
          fi

          # --- (MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) AI review of the WHOLE merged delta -------
          # The marker gate only proves feature TOKENS still exist, and the
          # build's (REFERR-GATE) only catches cannot-find-name diagnostics. A
          # cleanly-merging upstream hunk can still SEMANTICALLY break a fork
          # feature with zero conflicts — v1.14.2 rebuilt the sidebar row
          # projection in a non-conflicting hunk and silently dropped the fork's
          # snooze/archive/completed/deleted fields: merged clean, compiled
          # clean, broke Snooze + Recycle Bin + archived masters. EVERY merge —
          # clean or conflicted — is therefore reviewed by Opus against
          # FEATURES.md before it may commit/build/publish. Fail CLOSED: no
          # parsable verdict (rate limit, malformed output) aborts rather than
          # ships an unreviewed merge; a BREAKAGE verdict hard-aborts with the
          # findings in the log (baseline untouched -> recovery loop).
          echo "::group::semantic feature review (Opus)"
          npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
          REVIEW_FILE="$RUNNER_TEMP/merge-review.txt"
          rm -f "$REVIEW_FILE"
          # The reviewer must not modify the tree; snapshot the work-tree state
          # and refuse to continue if the review run changed ANYTHING.
          STATUS_BEFORE=$(git status --porcelain=v1 | sha256sum)
          REVIEW_PROMPT="IMPORTANT: Do NOT enter plan mode. You are reviewing an ALREADY-RESOLVED, staged, uncommitted merge of upstream superset tag ${NEWTAG} into our Windows-ARM64 fork. Your ONLY job is to decide whether the merged result SEMANTICALLY breaks any fork feature. Steps: (1) Read FEATURES.md — the fork feature manifest (feature descriptions + marker tokens). (2) Run 'git --no-pager diff --cached --name-only' to list every file the merge changed. (3) For each changed file that participates in a fork feature — it contains a FEATURES.md marker token, or it is in the blast radius of one (e.g. a projection/select/mapping that a feature's fields flow through, a component a feature's props pass through, a hook a feature's guard lives in) — read the staged diff ('git --no-pager diff --cached -- <file>') AND the surrounding file content, and verify the fork behaviour still works end-to-end: fork-added fields still projected/carried, fork branches and guards still reachable, fork props still passed, fork call sites still invoked. An upstream refactor that preserves behaviour is fine; flag only real breakage. (4) Write your verdict to the file at ${REVIEW_FILE} — the FIRST line must be exactly 'VERDICT: OK' or 'VERDICT: BREAKAGE'; for BREAKAGE add one line per finding: '<file> — <feature/marker> — <what broke>'. (5) If the file at ${WFDELTA_FILE} is non-empty, read it — it is upstream's CI/workflow delta we deliberately did NOT merge (.github/workflows is fork-owned). If it moves BUILD-RELEVANT logic into upstream CI (a codegen/generate step, a newly required env var, a packaging/signing step) that our fork's own build workflow would now silently miss, append one line per item after the verdict: 'CI-NOTE: <what upstream CI now does that the fork build may need>'. CI-NOTEs alone never change the verdict. Rules: do NOT edit ANY repository file; do NOT run git add / commit / checkout / merge / restore; only read files, run read-only git diff/log/show commands, and write the verdict file. Output nothing else."
          attempt=1
          while [ $attempt -le 2 ]; do
            echo "::group::claude review attempt $attempt"
            claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model opus --effort high -p "$REVIEW_PROMPT" || true
            echo "::endgroup::"
            if [ -s "$REVIEW_FILE" ] && head -1 "$REVIEW_FILE" | grep -qE '^VERDICT: (OK|BREAKAGE)$'; then break; fi
            attempt=$((attempt+1))
          done
          STATUS_AFTER=$(git status --porcelain=v1 | sha256sum)
          if [ "$STATUS_BEFORE" != "$STATUS_AFTER" ]; then
            echo "::error::(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) the review run modified the work tree — refusing to trust or commit it. Baseline untouched."
            exit 1
          fi
          if ! [ -s "$REVIEW_FILE" ] || ! head -1 "$REVIEW_FILE" | grep -qE '^VERDICT: (OK|BREAKAGE)$'; then
            echo "::error::(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) no parsable review verdict for $NEWTAG — refusing to ship an UNREVIEWED merge (fail closed). Baseline untouched."
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "--- review verdict ---"
          cat "$REVIEW_FILE"
          if head -1 "$REVIEW_FILE" | grep -q 'BREAKAGE'; then
            echo "::error::(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) Opus found fork-feature breakage in the $NEWTAG merge — rejecting. Findings above. Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline."
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "::endgroup::"

          # --- record new baseline + commit the merge -------------------------
          # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) The release tag is derived SOLELY from
          # apps/desktop/package.json by the build — if the merged version
          # doesn't match the tag being merged, the build would delete and
          # rebuild the WRONG release (e.g. a bad package.json conflict
          # resolution keeping 1.14.3 while merging desktop-v1.15.0), then
          # advance the baseline past a version that no longer has a correct
          # release. Assert the invariant here instead.
          VERSION=$(node -p "require('./apps/desktop/package.json').version")
          if [ "desktop-v${VERSION}" != "$NEWTAG" ]; then
            echo "::error::merged apps/desktop/package.json version ($VERSION) does not match $NEWTAG — bad version resolution; refusing to commit. Baseline untouched."
            exit 1
          fi
          # (WORKFLOW-FORK-OWNED) postcondition: the tree about to be committed
          # must contain ZERO workflow-dir changes — not from the merge, not
          # from the AI resolver, not from the review run. The restore step
          # above is the mechanism; this is the proof (a workflow delta would be
          # rejected by the push anyway — fail here, loud, with the baseline
          # untouched).

            exit 1
          fi

          git add -A
          git commit -m "merge upstream ${NEWTAG}" -m "Auto-merged by nightly-merge; conflicts resolved by Opus where present; all FEATURES.md markers verified present; merged delta passed the (MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) Opus feature review."
          # (CANDIDATE-IDEMPOTENT-PUSH) The candidate is a disposable CI branch:
          # re-derived from main each run, consumed by `build`, deleted by
          # `advance` ON SUCCESS only. A FAILED build never reaches `advance`, so
          # it strands the candidate on the remote. The next night re-merges the
          # SAME upstream version into a NEW commit (AI conflict resolution is
          # non-deterministic AND main may have advanced), whose history diverges
          # from the stale branch — a plain push is then rejected non-fast-forward
          # and EVERY subsequent nightly deadlocks on that stale ref. Drop any
          # stale remote copy first so the push is always clean. This is pure CI
          # scratch (never a feature/history branch), so overwriting it is safe;
          # we delete-then-push rather than --force a real branch.
          # (REHEARSAL) Everything above — merge, resolver, repair, every
          # gate, even the local merge commit — ran for real; stop before
          # anything leaves this runner. This is the on-demand emulation of
          # the overnight run: after ANY change to this workflow, dispatch
          # rehearse=true (+ force_tag / rehearse_base to replay a past
          # night) and get tonight's answer today, with zero side effects.
          if [ "$REHEARSE" = "true" ]; then

          echo "newtag=$NEWTAG" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "candidate=$CAND" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) build + advance consume this immutable SHA, not
          # the mutable branch name — a replaced candidate can't be built or
          # advanced unnoticed.
          echo "sha=$(git rev-parse HEAD)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "Merged $NEWTAG -> candidate $CAND @ $(git rev-parse HEAD) (version $VERSION). Building next."

  build:
    needs: merge
    if: needs.merge.outputs.should_build == 'true'
    uses: ./.github/workflows/build-arm64.yml
    with:
      # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) build the exact merge commit, immune to candidate-
      # branch mutation between jobs.
      ref: ${{ needs.merge.outputs.sha }}
      publish: true

  advance:
    needs: [merge, build]
    if: needs.merge.outputs.should_build == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - name: Fast-forward main to the verified candidate + clean up
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          CAND='${{ needs.merge.outputs.candidate }}'
          SHA='${{ needs.merge.outputs.sha }}'
          git fetch origin "$CAND"
          # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) advance ONLY the commit that was gated + built.
          if [ "$(git rev-parse FETCH_HEAD)" != "$SHA" ]; then
            echo "::error::candidate $CAND moved (expected $SHA, found $(git rev-parse FETCH_HEAD)) — another run interfered; refusing to advance main."
            exit 1
          fi
          # The candidate is main + the merge commit, so this is a fast-forward.
          git push origin "$SHA:refs/heads/main"
          git push origin --delete "$CAND"
          echo "Baseline advanced to ${{ needs.merge.outputs.newtag }}; Release published by the build job."

513	    if: needs.merge.outputs.should_build == 'true'
514	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
515	    steps:
516	      - name: Checkout
517	        uses: actions/checkout@v5
518	        with:
519	          fetch-depth: 0
520	      - name: Fast-forward main to the verified candidate + clean up
521	        shell: bash
522	        run: |
523	          set -euo pipefail
524	          CAND='${{ needs.merge.outputs.candidate }}'
525	          SHA='${{ needs.merge.outputs.sha }}'
526	          git fetch origin "$CAND"
527	          # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) advance ONLY the commit that was gated + built.
528	          if [ "$(git rev-parse FETCH_HEAD)" != "$SHA" ]; then
529	            echo "::error::candidate $CAND moved (expected $SHA, found $(git rev-parse FETCH_HEAD)) — another run interfered; refusing to advance main."
530	            exit 1
531	          fi
532	          # The candidate is main + the merge commit, so this is a fast-forward.
533	          git push origin "$SHA:refs/heads/main"
534	          git push origin --delete "$CAND"
535	          echo "Baseline advanced to ${{ needs.merge.outputs.newtag }}; Release published by the build job."

file.content:
# can NEVER push a .github/workflows/* change — GitHub rejects both
          # the candidate push and the main fast-forward with "refusing to allow
          # a GitHub App to create or update workflow … without `workflows`
          # permission" (desktop-v1.14.3 deploy-relay.yml incident: the merge,
          # gates and review all passed, then the push died). Restore our exact
          # workflow set BEFORE conflict detection: upstream-ADDED files are
          # dropped from index+worktree (--no-overlay), upstream edits to files
          # we long deleted resolve as ours, and the AI resolver never sees a
          # workflow path it could resurrect.
          #
          # UNMERGED paths must be cleared FIRST: `git checkout <tree> -- <dir>`
          # hard-errors ("path … is unmerged") on a modify/delete entry whose
          # file is absent in HEAD — upstream modifying a workflow we deleted
          # (desktop-v1.15.0 release-cli.yml incident: the nightly died right
          # here, before the resolver, two nights running). Resolve each
          # unmerged workflow path to OUR side: restore from HEAD when we carry
          # it, git rm when we deleted it.
          git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U -- .github/workflows | while IFS= read -r p; do
            if git cat-file -e "HEAD:$p" 2>/dev/null; then
              git checkout HEAD -- "$p"
            else
              git rm -q -f -- "$p"
            fi
          done
          git checkout --no-overlay HEAD -- .github/workflows

          CONFLICTS=$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U || true)
          if [ -n "$CONFLICTS" ]; then
            echo "::group::conflicts to resolve"; echo "$CONFLICTS"; echo "::endgroup::"
            npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
            PROMPT="IMPORTANT: Do NOT enter plan mode. You are inside a git repository mid-merge: upstream superset tag ${NEWTAG} is being merged into our Windows-ARM64 fork. ONLY the files listed by 'git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U' are conflicted. Resolve EVERY conflicted file: combine upstream's change with our fork's feature so BOTH intents survive and our feature keeps working; where upstream deliberately removed or refactored something we had only lightly touched, follow upstream. Do NOT edit any non-conflicted file. Do NOT weaken or remove any feature marker tracked in FEATURES.md. After resolving each file run 'git add <file>'. Do NOT commit. Do NOT run any other git command. When every conflict is resolved and staged, stop. Output nothing."
            attempt=1
            while [ $attempt -le 3 ]; do
              echo "::group::claude resolve attempt $attempt"
              claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model opus --effort high -p "$PROMPT" || true
              echo "::endgroup::"
              if [ -z "$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U || true)" ]; then break; fi
              attempt=$((attempt+1))
            done
            REMAIN=$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U || true)
            if [ -n "$REMAIN" ]; then
              echo "::error::Opus could not resolve all conflicts for $NEWTAG — recovery: fix locally and re-baseline. Remaining:"; echo "$REMAIN"
              exit 1
            fi
          else
            echo "Clean merge — no conflicts."
          fi

          # --- dependency-consistency gate + lock regen (LOCK-REGEN) ----------
          # The build installs --frozen-lockfile and electron-builder's
          # dependency traversal requires each workspace's DECLARED dep version
          # to be the one actually installed. A merge introduces two kinds of
          # drift that both kill the build ~25 min in (after a windows-arm64
          # runner spins up), not here:
          #   (1) a fork root `overrides` entry pins a package to a version
          #       DIFFERENT from the merged workspace pin (this is exactly how a
          #       stale {@mastra/core, mastracode} override broke every mastra
          #       bump: override forced 0.18.0, chat declared 0.18.1 ->
          #       electron-builder "production dependency not found 0.18.1"); and
          #   (2) the merge bumps a package.json dep but bun.lock still pins the
          #       old version (a stale frozen lock).
          # Catch (1) loud here (pure manifest analysis, platform-independent);
          # fix (2) by regenerating the lock with the build's exact bun version,
          # which ALSO fails loud if the merged manifests don't resolve or a pin
          # is age-blocked. Both run in this 2-min job, so the windows-arm64
          # build never spins up on a broken tree. We deliberately do NOT run a
          # full `--frozen-lockfile` install here: this is a linux runner, so a
          # platform-specific install/native-build quirk would be a false abort,
          # and after a successful --lockfile-only the lock is consistent with
          # the manifests by construction (same bun version the build uses).
          # Any failure below hard-aborts with the baseline untouched.
          echo "::group::dependency-consistency gate"
          node scripts/check-override-consistency.mjs
          echo "::endgroup::"

          echo "::group::regenerate bun.lock to match merged manifests"
          export SUPERSET_POSTINSTALL_RUNNING=1
          LOCKLOG="$RUNNER_TEMP/bun-lockfile.log"
          set +e
          bun install --lockfile-only > "$LOCKLOG" 2>&1
          rc=$?
          set -e
          cat "$LOCKLOG"
          if [ "$rc" -ne 0 ]; then
            if grep -q "blocked by minimum-release-age" "$LOCKLOG"; then
              echo "::error::Lock regen blocked by bunfig minimumReleaseAge — an upstream dep pin in $NEWTAG is <72h old. This is a timing race, not a breakage: the nightly will succeed once the version ages past the gate (re-run after it ages, or wait for tomorrow's schedule). Baseline left untouched."
            else
              echo "::error::bun install --lockfile-only failed (rc=$rc) after merging $NEWTAG — the merged manifests don't resolve. Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline."
            fi
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "::endgroup::"

          # --- (REFERR-REPAIR) run the build's ReferenceError gate HERE and let
          # Opus repair what it catches, so an import-dropping conflict
          # resolution self-heals tonight instead of failing the build and
          # costing a red night + manual recovery (desktop-v1.15.1
          # useChangesTab incident: the resolver took upstream's rewritten
          # import block, kept the fork's JSX, dropped Button/Tooltip/cn).
          # Repair is scoped HARD to cannot-find-name diagnostics; anything
          # still dirty after 2 attempts aborts exactly as before (the build
          # would have rejected it anyway — this just moves the gate earlier
          # and adds a bounded fix loop). --ignore-scripts keeps the linux
          # install pure-JS resolution (no native/postinstall platform quirks,
          # the reason this job deliberately avoids a full install elsewhere).
          echo "::group::(REFERR-REPAIR) ReferenceError gate + bounded AI repair"
          bun install --frozen-lockfile --ignore-scripts
          REFERRLOG="$RUNNER_TEMP/referr.log"
          run_referr_gate() {
            set +e
            node scripts/check-dangerous-diagnostics.mjs > "$REFERRLOG" 2>&1
            REFERR_RC=$?
            set -e
            cat "$REFERRLOG"
          }
          run_referr_gate
          # The repair agent must only EDIT the work tree — record the commit
          # state so an agent that disobeys "Do NOT commit" (advancing HEAD
          # and vacating the merge) is caught, not silently laundered into a
          # clean-looking tree.
          HEAD_BEFORE_REPAIR=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
          attempt=1
          while [ "$REFERR_RC" -ne 0 ] && [ $attempt -le 2 ]; do
            DIAGS=$(grep -F '(REFERR-GATE)' "$REFERRLOG" | head -40 || true)
            if [ -z "$DIAGS" ]; then
              # Gate failed WITHOUT naming diagnostics = the checker itself
              # broke (install/tsc infra). AI repair has nothing to act on.
              echo "::error::(REFERR-REPAIR) gate failed without diagnostics — checker infrastructure problem, not repairable. Log above. Baseline untouched."
              exit 1
            fi
            npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
            REPAIR_PROMPT="IMPORTANT: Do NOT enter plan mode. You are inside a git repository holding a staged, uncommitted merge of upstream superset tag ${NEWTAG}. The typecheck gate found cannot-find-name diagnostics — identifiers that would throw ReferenceError at runtime, almost always imports dropped during conflict resolution. Diagnostics: ${DIAGS} — Fix ONLY these diagnostics, and ONLY by ADDING code: add the missing import lines (match how the same symbols are imported elsewhere in this repo, e.g. @superset/ui/*) or restore the dropped declaration. NEVER delete, comment out, or rewrite existing code, JSX, or use-sites to silence a diagnostic — removing the code that uses the identifier is feature loss and is forbidden. Do NOT touch any file not named in the diagnostics. After editing each file run 'git add <file>'. Do NOT commit. Output nothing."
            echo "::group::claude referr-repair attempt $attempt"
            claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model opus --effort high -p "$REPAIR_PROMPT" || true
            echo "::endgroup::"
            run_referr_gate
            attempt=$((attempt+1))
          done
          if [ "$REFERR_RC" -ne 0 ]; then
            echo "::error::(REFERR-REPAIR) cannot-find-name diagnostics persist after repair attempts — aborting (the build would reject this tree). Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline. Baseline untouched."
            exit 1
          fi
          if [ "$(git rev-parse HEAD)" != "$HEAD_BEFORE_REPAIR" ] || ! git rev-parse -q --verify MERGE_HEAD >/dev/null; then
            echo "::error::(REFERR-REPAIR) the repair run committed or destroyed the merge state — refusing to trust it. Baseline untouched."
            exit 1
          fi
          # Stage any repair edits NOW: the semantic review below reads the
          # STAGED diff ('git diff --cached'); an unstaged repair would be
          # invisible to the review yet still swept into the final commit's
          # 'git add -A' — reviewed tree and shipped tree must be identical.
          git add -A
          echo "::endgroup::"

          # --- feature-marker gate (FEATURES.md) ------------------------------
          # Runs AFTER conflict resolution AND the REFERR repair loop, so it
          # judges the FINAL tree — a repair that "fixed" a diagnostic by
          # deleting fork code (forbidden, but prompts are not proofs) still
          # has to get past the marker grep here.
          echo "::group::feature-marker gate"
          # read the fenced ```markers block: lines are "<token><TAB><glob-root>"
          # Guard the guard: an empty/renamed block would pass VACUOUSLY (zero
          # markers checked == zero missing). Fail loud instead.
          MARKER_LINES=$(awk '/^```markers$/{f=1;next} /^```$/{f=0} f' FEATURES.md)
          if ! printf '%s\n' "$MARKER_LINES" | grep -q '[^[:space:]]'; then
            echo "::error::FEATURES.md markers block is missing or empty — the marker gate would pass vacuously. Refusing to trust this merge."
            exit 1
          fi
          printf '%s\n' "$MARKER_LINES" | while IFS=$'\t' read -r tok root; do
            [ -n "$tok" ] || continue
            if grep -rqsF -- "$tok" "$root" 2>/dev/null; then
              echo "  OK   $tok ($root)"
            else
              echo "::error::feature marker MISSING after merge: '$tok' under $root"
              echo missing >> "$RUNNER_TEMP/marker-missing"
            fi
          done
          echo "::endgroup::"
          if [ -f "$RUNNER_TEMP/marker-missing" ]; then
            echo "::error::A feature was dropped by the $NEWTAG merge — rejecting. Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline."
            exit 1
          fi

          # --- (MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) AI review of the WHOLE merged delta -------
          # The marker gate only proves feature TOKENS still exist, and the
          # build's (REFERR-GATE) only catches cannot-find-name diagnostics. A
          # cleanly-merging upstream hunk can still SEMANTICALLY break a fork
          # feature with zero conflicts — v1.14.2 rebuilt the sidebar row
          # projection in a non-conflicting hunk and silently dropped the fork's
          # snooze/archive/completed/deleted fields: merged clean, compiled
          # clean, broke Snooze + Recycle Bin + archived masters. EVERY merge —
          # clean or conflicted — is therefore reviewed by Opus against
          # FEATURES.md before it may commit/build/publish. Fail CLOSED: no
          # parsable verdict (rate limit, malformed output) aborts rather than
          # ships an unreviewed merge; a BREAKAGE verdict hard-aborts with the
          # findings in the log (baseline untouched -> recovery loop).
          echo "::group::semantic feature review (Opus)"
          npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
          REVIEW_FILE="$RUNNER_TEMP/merge-review.txt"
          rm -f "$REVIEW_FILE"
          # The reviewer must not modify the tree; snapshot the work-tree state
          # and refuse to continue if the review run changed ANYTHING.
          STATUS_BEFORE=$(git status --porcelain=v1 | sha256sum)
          REVIEW_PROMPT="IMPORTANT: Do NOT enter plan mode. You are reviewing an ALREADY-RESOLVED, staged, uncommitted merge of upstream superset tag ${NEWTAG} into our Windows-ARM64 fork. Your ONLY job is to decide whether the merged result SEMANTICALLY breaks any fork feature. Steps: (1) Read FEATURES.md — the fork feature manifest (feature descriptions + marker tokens). (2) Run 'git --no-pager diff --cached --name-only' to list every file the merge changed. (3) For each changed file that participates in a fork feature — it contains a FEATURES.md marker token, or it is in the blast radius of one (e.g. a projection/select/mapping that a feature's fields flow through, a component a feature's props pass through, a hook a feature's guard lives in) — read the staged diff ('git --no-pager diff --cached -- <file>') AND the surrounding file content, and verify the fork behaviour still works end-to-end: fork-added fields still projected/carried, fork branches and guards still reachable, fork props still passed, fork call sites still invoked. An upstream refactor that preserves behaviour is fine; flag only real breakage. (4) Write your verdict to the file at ${REVIEW_FILE} — the FIRST line must be exactly 'VERDICT: OK' or 'VERDICT: BREAKAGE'; for BREAKAGE add one line per finding: '<file> — <feature/marker> — <what broke>'. (5) If the file at ${WFDELTA_FILE} is non-empty, read it — it is upstream's CI/workflow delta we deliberately did NOT merge (.github/workflows is fork-owned). If it moves BUILD-RELEVANT logic into upstream CI (a codegen/generate step, a newly required env var, a packaging/signing step) that our fork's own build workflow would now silently miss, append one line per item after the verdict: 'CI-NOTE: <what upstream CI now does that the fork build may need>'. CI-NOTEs alone never change the verdict. Rules: do NOT edit ANY repository file; do NOT run git add / commit / checkout / merge / restore; only read files, run read-only git diff/log/show commands, and write the verdict file. Output nothing else."
          attempt=1
          while [ $attempt -le 2 ]; do
            echo "::group::claude review attempt $attempt"
            claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model opus --effort high -p "$REVIEW_PROMPT" || true
            echo "::endgroup::"
            if [ -s "$REVIEW_FILE" ] && head -1 "$REVIEW_FILE" | grep -qE '^VERDICT: (OK|BREAKAGE)$'; then break; fi
            attempt=$((attempt+1))
          done
          STATUS_AFTER=$(git status --porcelain=v1 | sha256sum)
          if [ "$STATUS_BEFORE" != "$STATUS_AFTER" ]; then
            echo "::error::(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) the review run modified the work tree — refusing to trust or commit it. Baseline untouched."
            exit 1
          fi
          if ! [ -s "$REVIEW_FILE" ] || ! head -1 "$REVIEW_FILE" | grep -qE '^VERDICT: (OK|BREAKAGE)$'; then
            echo "::error::(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) no parsable review verdict for $NEWTAG — refusing to ship an UNREVIEWED merge (fail closed). Baseline untouched."
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "--- review verdict ---"
          cat "$REVIEW_FILE"
          if head -1 "$REVIEW_FILE" | grep -q 'BREAKAGE'; then
            echo "::error::(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) Opus found fork-feature breakage in the $NEWTAG merge — rejecting. Findings above. Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline."
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "::endgroup::"

          # --- record new baseline + commit the merge -------------------------
          # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) The release tag is derived SOLELY from
          # apps/desktop/package.json by the build — if the merged version
          # doesn't match the tag being merged, the build would delete and
          # rebuild the WRONG release (e.g. a bad package.json conflict
          # resolution keeping 1.14.3 while merging desktop-v1.15.0), then
          # advance the baseline past a version that no longer has a correct
          # release. Assert the invariant here instead.
          VERSION=$(node -p "require('./apps/desktop/package.json').version")
          if [ "desktop-v${VERSION}" != "$NEWTAG" ]; then
            echo "::error::merged apps/desktop/package.json version ($VERSION) does not match $NEWTAG — bad version resolution; refusing to commit. Baseline untouched."
            exit 1
          fi
          # (WORKFLOW-FORK-OWNED) postcondition: the tree about to be committed
          # must contain ZERO workflow-dir changes — not from the merge, not
          # from the AI resolver, not from the review run. The restore step
          # above is the mechanism; this is the proof (a workflow delta would be
          # rejected by the push anyway — fail here, loud, with the baseline
          # untouched).
          if [ -n "$(git status --porcelain=v1 --untracked-files=all -- .github/workflows)" ]; then
            echo "::error::(WORKFLOW-FORK-OWNED) .github/workflows differs from HEAD after resolve/review — refusing to commit:"
            git status --porcelain=v1 --untracked-files=all -- .github/workflows
            exit 1
          fi
          printf '%s\n' "$NEWTAG" > .fork/upstream-baseline.txt
          git add -A
          git commit -m "merge upstream ${NEWTAG}" -m "Auto-merged by nightly-merge; conflicts resolved by Opus where present; all FEATURES.md markers verified present; merged delta passed the (MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) Opus feature review."
          # (CANDIDATE-IDEMPOTENT-PUSH) The candidate is a disposable CI branch:
          # re-derived from main each run, consumed by `build`, deleted by
          # `advance` ON SUCCESS only. A FAILED build never reaches `advance`, so
          # it strands the candidate on the remote. The next night re-merges the
          # SAME upstream version into a NEW commit (AI conflict resolution is
          # non-deterministic AND main may have advanced), whose history diverges
          # from the stale branch — a plain push is then rejected non-fast-forward
          # and EVERY subsequent nightly deadlocks on that stale ref. Drop any
          # stale remote copy first so the push is always clean. This is pure CI
          # scratch (never a feature/history branch), so overwriting it is safe;
          # we delete-then-push rather than --force a real branch.
          # (REHEARSAL) Everything above — merge, resolver, repair, every
          # gate, even the local merge commit — ran for real; stop before
          # anything leaves this runner. This is the on-demand emulation of
          # the overnight run: after ANY change to this workflow, dispatch
          # rehearse=true (+ force_tag / rehearse_base to replay a past
          # night) and get tonight's answer today, with zero side effects.
          if [ "$REHEARSE" = "true" ]; then
            echo "should_build=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            {
              echo "## REHEARSAL PASSED — $NEWTAG"
              echo "Merge + conflict resolution + REFERR-REPAIR + marker gate + dependency gates + semantic review all green."
              echo "Merge commit (local only, discarded): $(git rev-parse HEAD)"
              echo "No candidate pushed, no build, no advance, baseline untouched."
            } | tee -a "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
            exit 0
          fi
          git push origin --delete "$CAND" 2>/dev/null || true
          git push origin "$CAND"

          echo "should_build=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "newtag=$NEWTAG" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "candidate=$CAND" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) build + advance consume this immutable SHA, not
          # the mutable branch name — a replaced candidate can't be built or
          # advanced unnoticed.
          echo "sha=$(git rev-parse HEAD)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "Merged $NEWTAG -> candidate $CAND @ $(git rev-parse HEAD) (version $VERSION). Building next."

  build:
    needs: merge
    if: needs.merge.outputs.should_build == 'true'
    uses: ./.github/workflows/build-arm64.yml
    with:
      # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) build the exact merge commit, immune to candidate-
      # branch mutation between jobs.
      ref: ${{ needs.merge.outputs.sha }}
      publish: true

  advance:
    needs: [merge, build]
    if: needs.merge.outputs.should_build == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - name: Fast-forward main to the verified candidate + clean up
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          CAND='${{ needs.merge.outputs.candidate }}'
          SHA='${{ needs.merge.outputs.sha }}'
          git fetch origin "$CAND"
          # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) advance ONLY the commit that was gated + built.
          if [ "$(git rev-parse FETCH_HEAD)" != "$SHA" ]; then
            echo "::error::candidate $CAND moved (expected $SHA, found $(git rev-parse FETCH_HEAD)) — another run interfered; refusing to advance main."
            exit 1
          fi
          # The candidate is main + the merge commit, so this is a fast-forward.
          git push origin "$SHA:refs/heads/main"
          git push origin --delete "$CAND"
          echo "Baseline advanced to ${{ needs.merge.outputs.newtag }}; Release published by the build job."

questions.question: Architecture for the adaptation phase — how should the AI port fork code to upstream refactors?

options.label: Review-driven adapt loop (Recommended)
options.description: Keep mandatory Opus review; on BREAKAGE its findings drive a separate adaptation agent, then fresh review re-verifies. Max 3 rounds, fail-closed. Cost scales with actual breakage; reuses proven REFERR-REPAIR pattern. Tonight's findings were precise enough to fix from.

options.label: Proactive port pass + loop
options.description: Additionally, BEFORE the review, an agent analyses the upstream base→new API delta (renamed/deleted exports) and proactively adapts fork files referencing them. More AI cost every merge night; review loop still needed as backstop.

questions.question: If the desktop-v1.16.0 rehearsal goes green, run the real nightly (build + publish v1.16.0 Release + advance baseline) immediately after?

options.label: Yes, auto-run real nightly (Recommended)
options.description: Rehearsal green → dispatch real run same session. v1.16.0 ships tonight via the new pipeline. Install still waits for your go.

options.label: Rehearsal only, I decide after
options.description: Stop after green rehearsal; you review the adaptation diff in the run log before any publish.

Read the answers carefully — they may request clarification, changes, or that you not proceed — and follow what they actually say.

input.name: sol-merge-adapt
input.prompt:
You are implementing a redesign of the nightly upstream-merge workflow in the repo at "C:\Users\khair\Code Projects\fork-superset-windows-arm64" (a vendored Windows-ARM64 fork of superset-sh/superset). EDIT FILES ONLY — do NOT commit, do NOT push, do NOT run the workflow.

# Problem being solved (context)

The nightly merges upstream `desktop-v*` tags. git resolves the non-conflicting majority; an Opus agent resolves ONLY conflicted files. The structural flaw: upstream refactors of APIs that fork-only files *call* (but upstream never touched the fork files) merge with ZERO conflicts, so the resolver never sees them. The `(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE)` Opus review then detects the breakage precisely — but is forbidden from editing, so it can only abort. Result: every real upstream refactor night dies. Last night's concrete example (upstream desktop-v1.16.0 "local-first projects" refactor, all clean-merging):
- upstream deleted the `v2Projects` collection from `CollectionsProvider/collections.ts`; fork files `useKanbanData.ts:128`, `useKanbanActions.ts:656`, `PromoteCardDialog.tsx:75` still reference `collections.v2Projects`.
- upstream renamed `registerCloudAndLocal` → private unexported `registerLocalWorkspace` and deleted the `startHostEnsure` export from workspaces.ts; fork file `packages/host-service/src/trpc/router/workspace-creation/multi-repo-create.ts` still imports/calls both.

# The redesign (approved by maintainer): "(MERGE-ADAPT)"

Two additions to `.github/workflows/nightly-merge.yml` (535 lines, single big bash step `id: do` in job `merge`). Read the whole file first. Preserve EVERY existing guard and comment discipline (the file is heavily commented with incident history — keep that style; add comments explaining the new phases and referencing the v1.16.0 incident).

## Addition 1 — proactive port pass (after conflict resolution + the WORKFLOW-FORK-OWNED restore block, BEFORE the dependency-consistency gate)

A new AI pass that adapts fork-owned files to upstream's refactor even when nothing conflicted:

- Compute the fork-delta file list: files where the staged merged tree differs from the NEW upstream tag tree:
  `git diff --name-only --cached "refs/upstream-tags/$NEWTAG" > "$RUNNER_TEMP/fork-delta-files.txt"` — but EXCLUDE `.github/workflows/` paths and exclude `bun.lock`, `FEATURES.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `.fork/`. (These are the files that are fork-owned or fork-modified.)
- Run a claude invocation (same pattern as the existing resolver: `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model opus --effort high -p "$PROMPT" || true`, npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code first if not already installed at that point — note the resolver only installs it inside the CONFLICTS branch, so the port pass must ensure installation unconditionally).
- Prompt (single-quoted heredoc-safe; follow existing prompt style — imperative, explicit forbidden actions). Content requirements:
  - It is mid-merge of upstream tag $NEWTAG into the fork; previous upstream baseline tag is $BASE. Both available as `refs/upstream-tags/$BASE` and `refs/upstream-tags/$NEWTAG`.
  - Its job: find fork-owned files (list at $RUNNER_TEMP/fork-delta-files.txt) that reference upstream APIs/symbols/modules that CHANGED between $BASE and $NEWTAG (renamed/deleted/moved exports, changed signatures, removed collections/providers), using read-only git commands (`git diff refs/upstream-tags/$BASE refs/upstream-tags/$NEWTAG -- <path>`, `git show`) plus grep, and PORT the fork code to upstream's new API so the fork feature keeps working under the new upstream intent. Example framing: if upstream renamed/privatized an export the fork imports, rewire the fork call site to the new mechanism (or re-export it); if upstream deleted a collection the fork queries, migrate the fork query to upstream's replacement data source.
  - HARD rules: edit ONLY files listed in fork-delta-files.txt; never edit `.github/workflows/**`; never delete a fork feature or weaken any FEATURES.md marker; prefer minimal, surgical ports; `git add <file>` after each edit; do NOT commit; do NOT run merge/checkout/restore; if nothing needs porting, change nothing and stop.
- Guards after the pass (mirror the REFERR-REPAIR guard block): HEAD unchanged from before the pass, MERGE_HEAD still exists, else `::error` + exit 1. Then `git add -A` (but the workflow-dir postcondition later already catches workflow edits — also re-run the `git checkout --no-overlay HEAD -- .github/workflows` restore defensively after the pass, before `git add -A`, so a disobedient agent can't dirty the workflow dir).
- Wrap in `::group::(MERGE-ADAPT) proactive port pass`.
- Single attempt (no retry loop) — the review loop below is the backstop.

## Addition 2 — convert the semantic gate into a bounded detect→adapt→re-verify loop

Currently: review (2 attempts to get a parsable verdict) → BREAKAGE ⇒ exit 1. Replace with a loop, max 3 ROUNDS. Each round:
1. Review — exactly the existing REVIEW_PROMPT, review-attempt loop (2 attempts for a parsable verdict), and the STATUS_BEFORE/STATUS_AFTER "reviewer must not edit" sha256 check. No parsable verdict ⇒ exit 1 (fail closed, unchanged). Reviewer edited tree ⇒ exit 1 (unchanged).
2. `VERDICT: OK` ⇒ break out of the loop, proceed to commit.
3. `VERDICT: BREAKAGE` and rounds remain ⇒ adaptation invocation: pass the findings (all lines of the verdict file after line 1) into an ADAPT_PROMPT: mid-merge of $NEWTAG, an independent review found these fork-feature breakages, fix EACH finding by porting the fork code to upstream's new API/intent (again: may read upstream refs read-only to understand the refactor); never delete or stub out a fork feature to silence a finding; edit only files needed for the findings; never touch `.github/workflows/**`; `git add` each edited file; do NOT commit. Then: HEAD/MERGE_HEAD guard (same as above), defensive workflow-dir restore, `git add -A`, re-run `run_referr_gate` (the function already exists — NOTE its current definition may need to move earlier or stay usable here; it is defined in the REFERR-REPAIR section which runs before the review, so it's in scope) — if REFERR_RC nonzero after an adapt round, include the diagnostics text in the NEXT round's adapt prompt context or abort if it was the last round — simplest compliant behavior: append the referr diagnostics to the verdict-findings file so round N+1's adapt prompt sees them; also re-run the feature-marker gate (factor the existing marker-gate code into a bash function `run_marker_gate` used both in its current place and after each adapt round; a missing marker after an adapt round ⇒ immediate exit 1, no more rounds — a repair that deleted a feature is never acceptable).
4. `VERDICT: BREAKAGE` on the final round (no rounds left) ⇒ `::error` + exit 1 exactly like today (recovery: fix locally and re-baseline).

Important mechanics:
- The verdict file must be REMOVED/reset before each review round (`rm -f "$REVIEW_FILE"`), otherwise a stale verdict short-circuits.
- Keep `cat "$REVIEW_FILE"` + `--- review verdict ---` logging each round; add round numbering in group names: `::group::semantic review round $round` / `::group::(MERGE-ADAPT) repair round $round`.
- Fresh review each round means the same REVIEW_PROMPT (it reads the CURRENT staged diff each time; that is the fresh-eyes property).
- Total AI invocations worst case: 1 port + 3 reviews + 2 adapts. Acceptable.

## Other required edits

- Update the workflow's big header comment (lines 3–21) to describe the new architecture: merge → conflict resolve → proactive port pass → gates → review/adapt loop (bounded, fail-closed) → build. Mention the v1.16.0 incident as the motivating case.
- `FEATURES.md`: add a marker line to the fenced ```markers block: token `(MERGE-ADAPT)` with root `.github` (TAB-separated like the other lines — the gate parses `<token><TAB><root>`), and add a short feature description in the prose section near the (MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) description. The literal string `(MERGE-ADAPT)` must appear in `.github/workflows/nightly-merge.yml` (it will, via the group names/comments).
- `AGENTS.md`: update the "Nightly (minimal AI)" bullet and the "Gates" bullet in the fork-architecture section to describe the port pass + adapt loop (keep it terse, match existing style).

## Traps you MUST respect (from incident history)

- NEVER put an inline `${{ }}` expression inside the big `run:` script — GitHub caps expression-bearing scalars at 21000 chars and the whole workflow silently becomes unparseable. Dispatch inputs already come in via `env:`. Your additions must use plain bash vars only.
- The file must keep LF line endings (repo trap: CRLF breaks things; check with `git diff --check` and ensure no CR characters in edited files).
- Do not weaken/remove: NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY checks, WORKFLOW-FORK-OWNED restore + postcondition, LOCK-REGEN, REFERR-REPAIR, CANDIDATE-IDEMPOTENT-PUSH, marker-gate vacuous-pass guard, version↔tag assert, rehearsal semantics (rehearse=true still runs EVERYTHING including the new port pass + adapt loop, then stops before push/build/advance).
- `set -euo pipefail` is active; any command that may legitimately fail needs explicit handling (follow existing `set +e`/`set -e` patterns).
- Validate the final YAML parses: run `node -e "const yaml=require('js-yaml')..."` if js-yaml is available, or `python -c "import yaml,sys; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/nightly-merge.yml',encoding='utf-8'))"` via `uv run python` if pyyaml available — otherwise at minimum `npx --yes yaml-lint` or careful manual verification of indentation. Also sanity-check the bash with `bash -n` on the extracted script if feasible.

# Deliverable

Edited files: `.github/workflows/nightly-merge.yml`, `FEATURES.md`, `AGENTS.md`. Working tree only — no commits. Final report: summary of changes, how you validated YAML/bash syntax, any deviations from this spec and why.

content.text:
Sol is implementing the workflow redesign now. I'll review its diff when it finishes, then commit, push, and dispatch the rehearsal.

**Status so far:**
- Root cause confirmed: v1.16.0 upstream refactor merged clean, broke Kanban (`v2Projects` deleted) + multi-repo create (`startHostEnsure`/`registerCloudAndLocal` gone); semantic gate caught it but can only abort — no adaptation mechanism existed.
- Approved redesign in flight: proactive port pass + bounded review→adapt→re-verify loop (`MERGE-ADAPT`), all fail-closed guards kept.
- Next: my review → push to main → rehearse `desktop-v1.16.0` (loop must fix the breakage itself, no hand-patching) → on green, dispatch the real run.

hookInfos.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code stop'

content: Redesigning the nightly upstream-merge workflow so AI adapts fork code to upstream refactors instead of just aborting; Sol is implementing now. Next: I review its changes, push, then rehearse the failed v1.16.0 merge to prove it works. (disable recaps in /config)

Files:
- C:\Users\khair\Code Projects\fork-superset-windows-arm64\.github\workflows\nightly-merge.yml

- C:\Users\khair\Code Projects\fork-superset-windows-arm64\AGENTS.md

Implemented:
- Proactive `(MERGE-ADAPT)` port pass after conflict resolution and before dependency gates. It builds the upstream-vs-merged fork-delta list with all required exclusions, ensures Claude CLI is installed, invokes Opus/high once, guards HEAD + MERGE_HEAD, restores the fork-owned workflow tree, and stages the result.
- Factored `run_marker_gate` and reused it after every semantic adaptation.
- Replaced the one-shot semantic gate with max 3 fresh review rounds / max 2 repair rounds. Each review retains the existing 2-attempt parsable-verdict loop and reviewer-mutation hash guard; each repair receives all findings, guards merge state, restores workflows, stages, reruns REFERR + markers, carries actionable REFERR diagnostics forward, and fails closed at the bound.
- Updated header/rehearsal/merge-message documentation, added the `(MERGE-ADAPT)\t.github` marker + feature prose, and updated AGENTS architecture/AI/key-file text.

Validation passed:

- Extracted `jobs.merge.steps[id=do].run` passed `bash -n`.
- `git diff --check` clean (only core.autocrlf informational warnings).
- All three files verified LF-only (zero CR characters).
- Current FEATURES marker gate passes, protected fork-delta pathspec excludes all required classes, and the big merge `run:` scalar contains no inline `${{ }}` expressions.
- ShellCheck was unavailable and therefore skipped.

No spec deviations in implementation.
</teammate-message>

attachment.snippet:
1	<!-- ===================================================================== -->
2	<!-- FORK MAINTENANCE (khairm Windows ARM64 fork). Everything ABOVE the -->
3	<!-- divider is about maintaining this fork; everything BELOW is upstream  -->
4	<!-- superset's own developer guide, unchanged, for working on the app.    -->
5	<!-- ===================================================================== -->
6	
7	# superset-windows-arm64 — Windows ARM64 fork maintenance
8	
9	This repo is a **vendored fork** of [superset-sh/superset]: the full upstream
10	source with our Windows ARM64 + feature changes committed on top, plus CI that
11	builds a native **Windows ARM64** one-click installer. The fork is the source of
12	truth; we track upstream by **merging its deltas**, not by re-applying changes.
13	
14	## Fork architecture
15	
16	- **Baseline (frozen).** The whole fork is committed source. Building it is
17	  deterministic and **AI-free** — `.github/workflows/build-arm64.yml` does
18	  install → compile → materialize the win-arm64 native closure → package the NSIS
19	  installer → verify the packaged natives are ARM64. No upstream clone, no patch
20	  apply, no arch-fixup. `.fork/upstream-baseline.txt` records the upstream tag the
21	  baseline sits on.
22	- **Nightly (minimal AI).** `.github/workflows/nightly-merge.yml` runs nightly: if
23	  upstream published a newer `desktop-v*` tag, it `git merge`s it in. Git resolves
24	  the non-conflicting majority deterministically; **Opus** (`--effort high`)
25	  resolves conflicted files, then `(MERGE-ADAPT)` proactively ports fork-only
26	  callers across cleanly-merging upstream API refactors. AI cost scales with the
27	  **upstream delta per release**, NOT with how large the fork grows.
28	- **Gates.** After the merge/port: every marker in `FEATURES.md` must still be
29	  present (else a feature was dropped → reject), the dependency + ReferenceError
30	  gates run, then `(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE)` reviews the WHOLE merged delta. A
31	  BREAKAGE verdict drives a bounded `(MERGE-ADAPT)` repair followed by those gates
32	  and a fresh review; no verdict, reviewer edits, lost markers, or breakage after
33	  three reviews fail closed. Then the deterministic build must go green.
34	  On a clean merge + green build the nightly **auto-publishes the single
35	  `desktop-v<version>` Release** (rebuilt in place — see One-version below) and
36	  advances the baseline. Any failure (unresolvable conflict, lost marker,
37	  breakage verdict, build failure) **hard-aborts** and leaves the baseline
38	  untouched → recovery.
39	- **Recovery.** When a nightly merge can't be done cleanly, fix it locally with the
40	  maintainer, rebuild, re-freeze the baseline; the nightly then resumes merging only
41	  *new* upstream changes from that point.
42	
43	## Fork non-negotiables
44	
45	- **Whole feature set or fail loud.** Every `FEATURES.md` marker survives a merge or
46	  the build aborts. Never ship a partial fork.
47	- **v2-only, forever.** The v2 cloud/host-service stack is pinned on; never v1.
48	- **No build-time type/test gate — with ONE narrow exception.** The build runs
49	  electron-vite/esbuild; type/format errors don't fail it (the tree carries
50	  accepted type debt). The exception is `(REFERR-GATE)`
51	  (`scripts/check-dangerous-diagnostics.mjs`, run by the build workflow, and
52	  therefore by every nightly): cannot-find-name tsc diagnostics
53	  (TS2304/2552-class) DO fail the build — bare identifiers that throw
54	  ReferenceError at runtime, the exact damage a cleanly-merging upstream hunk
55	  leaves in fork code the AI resolver never sees (v1.14.0 layout.tsx
56	  incident). Validate + e2e locally before relying on a release.
57	- **AI only in the nightly merge.** The build is AI-free. The nightly needs
58	  `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN` for conflict resolution, the `(MERGE-ADAPT)` port/repair
59	  passes, and the mandatory `(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE)` reviews; if rate-limited it
60	  aborts rather than ship a half-merged, unported, or unreviewed fork.
61	- **One version, ever.** Exactly one GitHub Release per upstream version, tagged
62	  `desktop-v<version>`, **rebuilt in place** (delete + recreate so the tag always
63	  points at the latest build). NO `-beta`, NO prerelease, NO separate release/beta
64	  split — there is just *the* `desktop-v<version>` build.
65	
66	## Fork features (high-level + UX, for a full rebuild)
67	
68	See `FEATURES.md` for the marker manifest. In brief:
69	
70	- **Native Windows ARM64 packaging** — one-click NSIS installer; ARM64 node-pty,
71	  libsql, tokenizers; renderer CORS for `superset-app://`.
72	- **Window controls** — native `titleBarOverlay` is the sole min/max/close set on
73	  Windows, theme-matched; upstream's duplicate controls hidden on Windows.
74	- **Windows behaviour fixes** — skip quit-confirmation; cmd.exe fallback;
75	  force-foreground; hidden-window watchdog; WebGL first-paint recovery; Wispr Flow
76	  accessibility + paste fix (UIA reachable WITHOUT xterm screenReaderMode); fast
77	  non-blocking startup (no main-thread fs).
78	- **Agent status dots (Claude + Codex)** — a coloured dot per terminal + a workspace
79	  rollup: red = needs input, yellow = working (held while subagents run; also while
80	  Claude compacts context — PreCompact/SessionStart(source=compact) bracket it — and
81	  while agent-type background_tasks (teammates/forks/workflows) outlive the turn,
82	  and while a pid-alive codex-companion job for the session runs — held even
83	  through a Claude StopFailure, since codex is on its own API), green = ready
84	  for review, blue = a foreground shell command or a shell-only background
85	  remainder / cloud session (a manual /compact restores this blue from a
86	  turn-end snapshot marker instead of false-greening).
87	  Precedence red > yellow > blue > green. Host-service lifecycle POSTs + a JSONL
88	  watcher fallback; only open tabs are represented. superset-notify.py exclusively
89	  owns Claude's Stop — upstream's notify.sh is deliberately NOT registered on Stop
90	  (its raw passthrough raced ~1s behind and wiped BackgroundRunning blue + the
91	  subagent yellow-hold). Every dot surface (tab, pane header, sidebar row,
92	  sidebar agent chips (CHIP-DOT-UNIFY: status from the shared primitive,
93	  liveness from the open-pane gate — never `terminal_sessions.status`, which
94	  stays `active` forever for closed-tab ptys), workspace rollup, kanban card)
95	  derives from ONE per-source primitive in the
96	  v2-notifications store; the rollup is the fold of the per-source dots, so
97	  surfaces cannot drift. The primitive itself is layered (DOT-AXES): a source's
98	  status is DERIVED as the highest-precedence active axis (permission/working/
99	  review, plus the separate shell/background blue axes) — events latch/unlatch
100	  only the axes they have evidence about, so an assert can never stomp a
101	  higher-ranking active state; a tool that ran inside a subagent (hook payload
102	  carries agent_id, SUBTOOL-RED) asserts working WITHOUT clearing a pending
103	  red, while a main-loop tool completion still clears it (the answer proof).
104	  Leaked yellow-hold markers self-heal (MARKER-RECONCILE): a SubagentStop
105	  arriving with a mismatched/missing agent_id strands its run-dir marker and
106	  would pin yellow forever; every Stop/SubagentStop now reaps markers not
107	  listed as still running in the payload's background_tasks[] (ground truth).
108	  That payload is NOT ground truth for teammates though (TEAMMATE-IDLE):
109	  Claude Code reports finished teammates as status "running" forever, so
110	  superset-notify.py keeps an incremental per-terminal ledger of the lead
111	  transcript's teammate lifecycle (spawn/wake/message = active,
112	  idle_notification = idle) and drops teammate-type entries at a turn end
113	  once every tracked teammate is provably idle — a lead whose swarm has
114	  finished greens at its real final Stop instead of latching yellow until
115	  the next prompt (the 15-min .bgactive idle reap stays behind it for
116	  teammates that die without any transcript trace).
